macro_context.headline: Friday's tape: tech-led record-leg as TSLA +4%, AAPL +2%, NAS100 +2.35% — Iran-Hormuz remains the geopolitical wild card; weekend dollar drift continues, gold near record. Monday opens with US PPI later in the week as the next macro tell.

EURUSD.name: Euro / US Dollar
EURUSD.past: EUR/USD held flat near 1.1747 as a softer dollar (DXY -0.15%) was offset by a German factory orders miss; Iran-Hormuz peace headlines kept risk currencies bid against the buck.
EURUSD.why: The euro is riding the bearish-dollar trade J.P. Morgan flagged for 2026 — but the underlying European growth story is weak, capping upside. This is dollar weakness, not euro strength.
EURUSD.watch: ECB-speak this morning and US PPI tonight. A break above 1.1810 opens 1.1870; failure at 1.1700 hands control back to the dollar bears chasing 1.1620.

headlines.title: Sterling Today: Pound jumps as dollar sells off on Iran-Hormuz deal hopes
Oil Shock + Hawkish Fed = Major EUR/USD Downside? Don't Miss These Trading Opportunities
Eurozone inflation hits 3% as oil prices spike and economic growth slows

GBPUSD.name: British Pound / US Dollar
GBPUSD.past: Sterling drifted to 1.3581 (-8 pips) — the pound's recent dollar-weakness rally stalled as BoE hold expectations and softer UK data capped the bid above 1.36.
GBPUSD.why: GBP is the cleanest expression of the weak-dollar trade with no domestic catalyst — meaning it moves on DXY direction, not UK fundamentals. That makes it vulnerable if the dollar bounces.
GBPUSD.watch: BoE rate decision next week is the binary. Today: 1.36 is the line — hold it and 1.3680 is open; lose it and 1.3500 stops are exposed.

headlines.title: Yen Spikes to 10-Week High and Sparks Intervention Chatter
UK Elections Threaten to End the Pound’s Resilient Run
Sterling Today: Pound jumps as dollar sells off on Iran-Hormuz deal hopes

USDJPY.name: US Dollar / Japanese Yen
USDJPY.past: USD/JPY ground higher to 156.84 (+21 pips) despite broad dollar weakness — yen unable to capitalize as MoF intervention chatter cooled and 10Y yields backed up to 4.39%.
USDJPY.why: The yen is trapped: every dollar leg-down should help, but BoJ glacial pace plus 4%+ US yields keeps carry alive. Intervention is the only real downside risk now.
USDJPY.watch: 157.00 is the line in the sand for MoF. Break it and verbal warnings escalate within hours; hold below and 155.00 support comes back into play on any risk-off flare.

headlines.title: Why a Plunging Dollar Is Missing Out on the Relief Rally
How Markets Reacted to Trump's Iran Post, in Charts
The Dollar Isn’t Done. Why It’s Outshining Rivals Amid Iran Crisis.

AUDUSD.name: Australian Dollar / US Dollar
AUDUSD.past: AUD/USD slipped to 0.7226 (-13 pips) — the aussie failed to keep pace with the broader dollar selloff as iron ore softness and China demand worries weighed.
AUDUSD.why: AUD is the textbook risk-on commodity proxy, but with copper pulling back and China stimulus disappointing, the typical 'weak dollar = strong AUD' link is breaking down.
AUDUSD.watch: RBA meeting minutes Tuesday. Today watch 0.7200 — a decisive break opens 0.7150; a reclaim of 0.7280 puts 0.7340 back in play for the dollar bears.

headlines.title: The Dollar is Now Lower Than When the Iran War Began
Dollar softens as Middle East ceasefire holds
Why is the Australian Dollar Rallying?

XAUUSD.name: Gold
XAUUSD.past: Gold pushed +0.54% to $4,725, trading near record territory as Iran-Hormuz tension flared back up and US 10Y real yields pulled back. Silver outperformed at +0.80%.
XAUUSD.why: Gold is doing both jobs at once: hedging a real-yield slip AND a geopolitical re-escalation. With central bank buying intact, the macro setup remains as bullish as it gets.
XAUUSD.watch: $4,800 is the round-number magnet. A US PPI miss and any Iran headline keeps gold on the bid; a hawkish surprise + Iran de-escalation is the only path back below $4,650.

headlines.title: India demand subdued on higher prices, China premiums hold firm
A Look At Lundin Gold (TSX:LUG) Valuation After Strong Q1 2026 Results And Record Free Cash Flow
Gold Maintains Positive Structure, Technical Analysis Shows

XAGUSD.name: Silver
XAGUSD.past: Silver pushed +1.46% to $80.86, leading precious metals on Friday as gold also tagged a fresh leg higher; AI-datacenter and solar demand keeps the physical squeeze story alive.
XAGUSD.why: Silver continues to do double duty — safe-haven AND industrial — and the gold-silver ratio compression has further to run. The structural deficit narrative is now consensus.
XAGUSD.watch: $82 is the next clean break for fresh highs and a momentum chase. A weekend Iran de-escalation headline is the only thing that drags it back below $78 short term.

headlines.title: Hecla Mining (HL) Valuation Check After Record Free Cash Flow And Shift To A Debt Free Balance Sheet
Does Manuka’s Shift Toward Pipeline Ridge Drilling Reframe Its Gold Strategy for ASX:MKR Investors?
Endeavour Silver Q1 Earnings Call Highlights

USOIL.name: US Crude Oil (WTI)
USOIL.past: WTI added +0.31% to $95.10 as US-Iran clash headlines reignited Hormuz risk premium; the futures curve remains in steep backwardation signaling tight physical.
USOIL.why: Oil is trading entirely on the Iran binary now — the demand/supply fundamentals are noise. Every peace-deal headline whips it lower, every clash report sends it back up.
USOIL.watch: $93 support is critical — a peace deal headline likely sends WTI to $88 fast. A confirmed strike or shipping disruption opens the $100+ panic zone.

headlines.title: Car owners don't want to trade down on auto maintenance: Valvoline CEO
Maybe it's not so bad this market rally doesn't feel so euphoric
British Airways’ parent company expects £1.7bn hit from higher fuel bill

US500.name: S&P 500
US500.past: S&P 500 slipped -0.38% to 7,337 from record-high territory — a tech-led stealth rally cooled as Iran-US tensions re-flared and yields backed up to 4.39%.
US500.why: The April +10% melt-up was AI capex narrative meeting positioning unwinds. Now we're in the digestion phase: every record needs a fresh catalyst, and Iran is taking liquidity out.
US500.watch: PPI tonight is the next catalyst. 7,300 is now the inflection — hold it and dip-buyers reload; lose it and 7,200 gap-fill becomes the magnet for week's end.

headlines.title: Tech-led stealth rally pushes markets to record highs
The stock market's semi-charmed life: Morning Brief
Oil climbs on Gulf clashes as AI drives stocks to weekly rise

NAS100.name: Nasdaq 100
NAS100.past: Nasdaq 100 ripped +2.35% to 29,235 on Friday — best session in weeks as TSLA surged 4%, AAPL added 2%, NVDA +1.75%, all bidding tech back into record territory on the AI-capex tape.
NAS100.why: This is mega-cap tech reasserting leadership: every datacenter/compute headline (Anthropic-SpaceX) is direct earnings read-through. Index-level move means money is rotating IN, not chasing leadership.
NAS100.watch: 29,000 is now the floor to defend. Hold it Monday and 29,500 opens; lose it and 28,500 gap-fill is on. Watch for any weekend Iran de-escalation that triggers profit-taking on the geopolitical hedge.

headlines.title: Louis Navellier sends powerful message on AI FOMO trade, economy
The Nasdaq's top winners are now running hotter than in 2000: Chart of the Day
Hot Mega IPOs Like SpaceX and Anthropic Could Land In Your Index Funds Sooner Than You Think

US30.name: Dow Jones Industrial Average
US30.past: Dow underperformed at -0.63% to 49,597 — cyclicals and energy mixed as oil rose on Iran but rate-sensitive industrials sold off with the 10Y yield bid.
US30.why: The Dow is the index that hates this regime — high yields hurt the rate-sensitive industrials, and AI doesn't bail it out the way it does Nasdaq. It's the cleanest 'old economy' tell.
US30.watch: 49,000 is the line; lose it and the underperformance vs Nasdaq accelerates. Reclaiming 50,000 needs a yield drop — PPI tonight is the catalyst either way.

headlines.title: Dow Jones Futures: Oil Prices Rise As U.S., Iran Clash; Rocket Lab, Cloudflare, IREN Are Big Earnings Movers Late
Small-Cap Stocks Are Plunging. Are Stalled Iran Negotiations Responsible?
Wall Street ends lower as chip stocks give up gains

BTC.name: Bitcoin
BTC.past: Bitcoin fell -0.94% to $79,259 — risk-off bleed as equities sold and dollar bounced intraday; BTC dominance climbed to 58.31% as alts underperformed.
BTC.why: BTC is no longer trading as gold-correlation; it's a high-beta NDX trade. The crypto-mortgage and ETF inflow narrative is intact, but short-term it follows risk appetite, not gold.
BTC.watch: $78,000 is the support. Below it, $75K becomes the magnet. Above $81K and the breakout to fresh highs at $85K opens up — equity tape sets the tone.

headlines.title: Crypto-backed mortgages are hitting the mainstream. Here's how they work.
Wall Street has a new way to chase the AI memory boom
Why Is The Crypto Market Down Today?

ETH.name: Ethereum
ETH.past: Ethereum dropped -1.03% to $2,268 — underperforming BTC as alt-season indicators flipped negative and the L2 narrative cooled with risk-off equity flows.
ETH.why: ETH/BTC ratio is at multi-year lows; until it stops bleeding, ETH lacks an independent bid. Spot-ETF flows have been net-neutral, removing the structural support BTC enjoys.
ETH.watch: $2,200 is the panic line — break it and $2,000 round-number is target. A reclaim of $2,350 with rising volume signals the alt-rotation finally starting.

headlines.title: Bitcoin and ethereum prices today, Thursday, May 7, 2026: Bitcoin holds near $81,000 this morning
Report finds Solana developer share jumps to 23% as Ethereum weakens
Is This Sign a Big Red Flag for Ethereum?

NVDA.name: NVIDIA
NVDA.past: NVIDIA closed +1.75% at $215.20 Friday — fresh record territory as the Anthropic-SpaceX compute deal narrative kept hyperscaler demand center stage and order-book scrutiny built ahead of earnings.
NVDA.why: Every new frontier-lab + hyperscaler deal feeds directly into NVDA's 2027 backlog. The market is now pricing zero supply slack, so any earnings-call commentary on capacity will move the tape.
NVDA.watch: Earnings in 2 weeks is the binary. Today: $218 is breakout territory; clear it and $225 opens. Below $210 and the AI-capex skeptics get courage back.

headlines.title: Markets 'love chasing bottlenecks': Wall Street weighs epic run in AI stocks
The $1 trillion club's new members are powering the AI boom: Chart of the Day
My Top 3 Financial Stocks for May 2026

TSLA.name: Tesla
TSLA.past: Tesla ripped +4.02% to $428.35 Friday — best mega-cap performer as robotaxi deployment headlines combined with squeeze flows; no specific catalyst, just narrative momentum and OPEX-week gamma.
TSLA.why: TSLA still trades on optionality, not numbers. At 50x earnings the move size is positioning, not fundamentals — but the narrative compounding (robotaxi/AI/energy) is hard to short.
TSLA.watch: $430 is the gamma-magnet for next week. Hold $420 Monday and the squeeze tape extends; lose it and the leveraged longs get flushed back to $400 fast.

headlines.title: The $1 trillion club's new members are powering the AI boom: Chart of the Day

Tesla (TSLA): Top 10 Battery Technology Stock To Buy

AAPL.name: Apple
AAPL.past: Apple added +2.05% to $293.32 Friday — services growth narrative reasserted itself, Trump-EU tariff extension headline removed a left-tail risk, and buyback bid was constant under the stock.
AAPL.why: AAPL is now the defensive mega-cap — boring is the bull case. Lowest-beta way to stay long Big Tech, with buybacks supporting the tape and no AI-capex hangover to digest.
AAPL.watch: $295 is the next breakout level. WWDC keynote in 4 weeks is the next narrative catalyst; below $288 and the bid weakens. Watch how it trades on any Monday risk-off open.

headlines.title: Markets 'love chasing bottlenecks': Wall Street weighs epic run in AI stocks
The $1 trillion club's new members are powering the AI boom: Chart of the Day
Dow Jones Futures: Iran Responds To U.S. Deal Offer, Trump-Xi Summit Ahead; Apple, Nvidia, Boeing In Buy Areas

MSFT.name: Microsoft
MSFT.past: Microsoft slipped -1.34% to $415.12 Friday — the AI-capex skeptics gained traction as Azure cost commentary from peer software names raised margin questions; broader tech rally couldn't keep MSFT bid.
MSFT.why: MSFT is the cleanest AI-infrastructure bellwether and it's diverging from the rest of Mag7 on margin worries. Azure consumption growth into 2027 is the only thing that matters.
MSFT.watch: $415 is the line — hold it Monday and the AI-capex story stays intact; lose it and $410 opens. Reclaim of $420 needed to put $425 breakout back in play.

headlines.title: Markets 'love chasing bottlenecks': Wall Street weighs epic run in AI stocks
The $1 trillion club's new members are powering the AI boom: Chart of the Day

META.name: Meta Platforms
META.past: Meta dropped -1.16% to $609.63 Friday — ad-revenue resilience couldn't offset Reality Labs cost worries; some rotation OUT of META and INTO Apple/Tesla as the mega-cap leadership shuffled.
META.why: META's AI-capex spend remains the most scrutinized in mega-cap. Every margin-questioning headline at peer names amplifies the spend-vs-return debate that defines the bear case.
META.watch: $605 is the support line — lose it and $595 is the next stop. Reclaim $620 and fresh highs come back. Monday open will tell you if the Friday rotation was technical or thematic.

headlines.title: Markets 'love chasing bottlenecks': Wall Street weighs epic run in AI stocks
The $1 trillion club's new members are powering the AI boom: Chart of the Day
Should You Buy CoreWeave (CRWV) on Post-Earnings Dip?

GOOGL.name: Alphabet (Class A)
GOOGL.past: Alphabet finished flat at $397.99 — Gemini momentum solid but Search disruption headlines from AI-native competitors weighed, with antitrust overhang persisting.
GOOGL.why: GOOGL is the most macro-sensitive Mag7 because of the antitrust DOJ-remedies tail risk. Until that resolves, it trades at a compressed multiple to the rest of the cohort.
GOOGL.watch: $400 round-number is the resistance. A clear break opens $415; below $390 and the stock retests $380 where the antitrust selling last bottomed.

headlines.title: Anthropic taps SpaceX data center for AI compute power
Stocks take a breather after reaching record highs
Netlist Stock: The Next 10-Bagger?

AMZN.name: Amazon
AMZN.past: Amazon dropped -1.39% to $271.17 — AWS growth concerns from a competitor analyst note plus retail margin worries on tariff pass-through pressured shares.
AMZN.why: AMZN's two engines diverged this print: AWS deceleration vs Azure/GCP is the bear case, retail tariff exposure is the macro overhang. Both are durable themes.
AMZN.watch: $270 is the support — lose it and $263 is the gap-fill target. AWS re:Invent in 6 months is the next narrative catalyst; until then it trades on macro risk-off flows.
